message:
chore: minor fixes and test updates across fcp crates

Small fixes and documentation additions from concurrent development:

- connectors/twitter: Remove duplicate export
- crates/fcp-cli: Add missing doc comments
- crates/fcp-core: Fix operation test vector, add policy doc
- crates/fcp-host: Add discovery module stub
- crates/fcp-registry: Add missing export
- crates/fcp-testkit: Add policy simulation test
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Sync tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -915,7 +915,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff.3","title":"fcp.twitter: Read Operations (search/timelines/lookups)","description":"# fcp.twitter: Read Operations (search/timelines/lookups)

## Goal
Implement read-only operations:
- search
- tweet fetch
- user lookup
- timelines
- trends

## Requirements
- Capability gated.
- Taint inbound content as external input.
- Strong redaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff.4","title":"fcp.twitter: Write Operations (tweets/DMs)","description":"# fcp.twitter: Write Operations (tweets/DMs)

## Goal
Implement publishing operations:
- create/reply/thread/delete/retweet/like
- DMs (high sensitivity)

## Requirements
- Capability gated.
- Side effects emit receipts/audit.
- Dangerous operations require ApprovalToken `Execution` scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff.5","title":"fcp.twitter: Streaming Ingestion (filtered stream)","description":"# fcp.twitter: Streaming Ingestion (filtered stream)

## Goal
Consume the filtered stream API and emit EventEnvelopes.

## Requirements
- Singleton-writer lease for any long-lived stream connection.
- Backpressure and bounded buffers.
- Strict parsing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff.7","title":"E2E: Twitter/X Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Twitter/X Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add Twitter/X scenarios to the shared compliance harness.

## Scenarios
- Default deny.
- Allow with valid token.
- Network guard allow/deny.
- Streaming connection behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features twitter` passes deterministically.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yr0","title":"fcp.figma: Comment thread management","description":"# fcp.figma: Comment thread management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to manage Figma comment threads for design review workflows:
- list comments
- create comment
- reply
- resolve/unresolve

## Scope
### Read
- List comments for a file (pagination).

### Write (sensitive)
- Create comment on a node.
- Reply to a thread.
- Resolve/unresolve threads.
- Delete comment (dangerous; optional).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.comments.read`
- `figma.comments.write` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `figma.comments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Comments contain human text and may contain sensitive info:
  - treat as tainted
  - never log raw comment bodies
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include file key + comment id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.comment.list`
  - `figma.comment.create`
  - `figma.comment.reply`
  - `figma.comment.resolve`
  - Optional: `figma.comment.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - write flows + audit/receipt
  - permission denied
  - log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Comment operations are safe, auditable, and do not leak content into logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yvul","title":"TEST: fcp-graphql Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-graphql Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-graphql` via comprehensive deterministic unit tests against mocked schemas/endpoints, with structured logs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for GraphQL client infrastructure.

## Test Categories

### Query Execution
- [ ] Simple query execution
- [ ] Query with variables
- [ ] Mutations
- [ ] Subscriptions setup

### Query Building
- [ ] Fragment composition
- [ ] Nested selections
- [ ] Aliases
- [ ] Directives (@include, @skip)

### Pagination
- [ ] Cursor-based (Relay) pagination
- [ ] Offset-based pagination
- [ ] Automatic page traversal
- [ ] Total count handling

### Error Handling
- [ ] GraphQL errors parsing
- [ ] Partial data handling
- [ ] Network errors
- [ ] Timeout handling

### Batching & Deduplication
- [ ] Request batching
- [ ] Query deduplication
- [ ] Cache integration

### Schema Introspection
- [ ] Schema fetching
- [ ] Type validation
- [ ] Deprecation handling

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock GraphQL endpoints with wiremock
- Test against Linear-like, GitHub-like schemas
- Inject various error conditions

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.
